memoryArtifacts.headline: Ambrose FOWLER, from NEHGR V-11, pg 2

memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Fowler, Fouler, Fowlers, Fouler, Fowlar, Folar, Fouller, Fowlare, Foweller, Fowaller, Foulier, Foullar, Foular and many more. First found in Norfolk where they were seated from very ancient times, some say well before the Norman Conquest and the arrival of Duke William at Hastings in 1066 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: John Fowler who settled in Virginia in 1622; just two years after the arrival of the "Mayflower." He was followed soon after by settlers of the name in Barbados, Massachusetts, Maryland, and Rhode Island. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: He is wise who watches. This interesting surname is of Anglo-Saxon origin, and is from an occupational name for a bird-catcher or hunter of wild birds. In the medieval period a fowler would have been an important position, and all major houses would have employed one. The derivation is from the Old English pre-7th Century "fugelere", hunter of wild birds. Job-descriptive surnames originally denoted the actual occupation of the name bearer, and later became hereditary. There are several name bearers listed in the "Dictionary of National Biography", many being men of the church, the others tending towards innovation, such as John Fowler (1826 - 1864), who invented the steam plow, in 1858 he received a prize from the Royal Agricultural Society for his steam cultivator, and William Fowler (1761 - 1832), an artist, who was said to have introduced lead lines in representing colored glass. The name reached the New World in the early 17th Century, when it is recorded that one George Fowler, aged 22 yrs., sailed aboard the "Primrose" in July 1635, bound for Virginia. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Richard Fugelere, which was dated 1218, witness in the "Assize Court Rolls of Lancashire", during the reign of King Henry 2nd, known as "The Frenchman", 1216 - 1272. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.New Haven, Connecticut Colony: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文
zh.lifeSummary: 當Judge William Fowler I of Milford於大約1598年出生在英國德比夏爾達爾波利時，他的父親William Fowler50歲，母親Ann Heywood41歲。他於1620年與Sarah Tapp在英國德比夏爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1638年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼。他於1639年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂被列為judge。他去世於1660年1月25日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文米爾福德享年63歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 哈文米爾福德米爾福德 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Judge William Fowler I of Milford于大约1598年出生在英国德比夏爾達爾波利时，他的父亲William Fowler50岁，母亲Ann Heywood41岁。他于1620年与Sarah Tapp在英国德比夏爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1638年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼。他于1639年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂被列为judge。他去世于1660年1月25日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文米爾福德享年63岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文米爾福德米爾福德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Fowler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a fowler, a hunter or trapper of wild birds (a common medieval occupation), from Middle English fogheler, fugheler (Old English fugelere, a derivative of fugol ‘bird’).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Judge William Fowler I of Milford was born about 1598, in Dalbury, Derbyshire, England, his father, William Fowler, was 50 and his mother, Ann Heywood, was 41. He married Sarah Tapp in 1620, in Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He immigrated to Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1638. In 1639, at the age of 42, his occupation is listed as judge in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. He died on 25 January 1660, in Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 63, and was buried in Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Judge William Fowler I of Milford est né environ 1598, à Dalbury, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, William Fowler, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Ann Heywood, avait 41 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Tapp en 1620, à Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il a immigré au Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1638. En 1639, à l’âge de 42 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est judge à Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Il est décédé le 25 janvier 1660, à Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Connecticut, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Judge William Fowler I of Milford è nato il circa 1598 nel Dalbury, Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, William Fowler, aveva 50 anni e il suo madre, Ann Heywood, aveva 41 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Tapp nel 1620, nel Derbyshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Immigrato nel Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1638. Nel 1639, all'età di 42 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come judge nel Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Morì il 25 gennaio 1660, nel Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Judge William Fowler I of Milfordが約1598年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアダルブリーで生まれた時，父William Fowlerは50歳，母Ann Heywoodは41歳であった。1620年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアでSarah Tappと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1638年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーに移住した。1639年，42歳の時，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニューヘイベン トウンでの職業はjudgeと記録されている。彼は1660年1月25日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンミルフォードで63歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンミルフォードミルフォード セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Judge William Fowler I of Milford(이)가 대략 1598년 영국 더비실르 다버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Fowler(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Heywood(은)는 41세였다. 그는 1620년 영국 더비실르에서 Sarah Tapp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1638년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이로 이민을 떠났다. 1639년, 42세의 나이로 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운에서 judge이었다. 그는 1660년 1월 25일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 미퍼드에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 미퍼드 미퍼드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Judge William Fowler I of Milford nasceu aproximadamente 1598, em Dalbury, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Fowler, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Ann Heywood, tinha 41 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Tapp em 1620, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1638. Em 1639, com 42 anos, sua profissão aparece como judge em Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu em 25 de janeiro de 1660, em Milford, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Judge William Fowler I of Milford родился около 1598 г. в местности далбури, дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Fowler было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Ann Heywood, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Tapp в 1620 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он иммигрировал в массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1638 году. В 1639 году, в возрасте 42 лет,  он работал по профессии judge в местности нюхавен тоун, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер 25 января 1660 г. в местности милфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности милфорд кеметири, милфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Judge William Fowler I of Milford nació aproximadamente 1598, en Dalbury, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Fowler, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Ann Heywood, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Sarah Tapp en 1620, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Inmigró a Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1638. En 1639, a la edad de 42 años, su ocupación registrada era judge en Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Murió el 25 de enero de 1660, en Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
